puzzles.title: Sum Two Numbers
puzzles.prompt: Complete the function so it returns the sum of a and b.
puzzles.narrative: Simple. Clean. The world is still mostly intact.
hints: a function can return a value with the return keyword
you have two parameters — what do you do with them?
return a + b

puzzles.title: Reverse a String
puzzles.prompt: Finish the function to reverse the input string.
puzzles.narrative: Something in the world flipped. You almost didn't notice.
hints: strings can be iterated in reverse
std::reverse exists in <algorithm>
or loop from s.length()-1 down to 0

puzzles.title: Count Characters
puzzles.prompt: Return how many characters are in the string without using s.length().
puzzles.narrative: Count them yourself. The easy way is gone. Did you see it leave?
hints: a loop can iterate over each character
increment a counter for each character
stop when you hit the null terminator or end of string

puzzles.title: Find Max in Array
puzzles.prompt: Complete the function to return the largest number in the array.
puzzles.narrative: Find the largest thing left. There is less of everything now.
hints: start by assuming the first element is the max
loop through the rest and compare
update your max when you find something larger

puzzles.title: Fibonacci
puzzles.prompt: Return the nth Fibonacci number using recursion.
puzzles.narrative: A function that calls itself. The world does this now too.
hints: base cases: fib(0) = 0, fib(1) = 1
every other case calls fib twice
return fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)

puzzles.title: Palindrome Check
puzzles.prompt: Return true if the string is a palindrome, false otherwise.
puzzles.narrative: It reads the same forwards and backwards. Nothing else does anymore.
hints: compare the string to its reverse
or use two pointers — one from each end
move inward until they meet

puzzles.title: Pointer Swap
puzzles.prompt: Use pointers to swap the values of a and b.
puzzles.narrative: The values changed places. You didn't see it happen. Nothing does anymore.
hints: a pointer holds a memory address — dereference with *
you need a temporary variable to hold one value during the swap
int temp = *a; *a = *b; *b = temp;

puzzles.title: Class: Rectangle
puzzles.prompt: Finish the Rectangle class so area() returns width * height.
puzzles.narrative: A class. A blueprint for something that no longer exists cleanly.
hints: the class already has w and h as member variables
area() needs to return a value
return w * h

puzzles.title: Fix the Memory Leak
puzzles.prompt: Fix the function so it correctly allocates, uses, and frees memory without leaking or causing undefined behavior.
puzzles.narrative: Memory allocated. Memory forgotten. The world knows this feeling well by now.
hints: a reference is declared with & — int& r = *p
increment through the reference, not the pointer
save the value before you delete, then return the saved value
